text:
Now I have the full picture. Let me assess this carefully.

**Key findings:**
- **Event:** `radio_transcript_flag` (guard radio transcription triggered a flag) — E1 level
- **Prior:** 0.5 with n=0 (hand-set assumption, no actual data backing it)
- **Precedents:** Zero — this is novel for this site/zone
- **Board load:** **20 items/10min** — well past "flood" conditions (threshold is 6)
- **Active rule R-004:** Under flood conditions (>6/10min), suppress E0-E1 to protect operator

The operator is drowning at 20 items. R-004 is explicit — suppress E0-E1 in flood conditions. A `radio_transcript_flag` at E1 with no corroboration and no precedent doesn't warrant stealing the operator's attention from potentially more serious items already on their board. I'll suppress with a TTL so it can re-float later if conditions improve.

function.name: make_decision
function.arguments: {"chosen_move": "commit", "action_id": "suppress_ttl", "prior_adjustment_log_odds": -0.5, "adjustment_reasons": ["Board load at 20/6 — flood conditions, Rule R-004 mandates suppress E0-E1", "Prior is hand-set (n=0) with no learned weight, freeing adjustment downward", "No precedents at this site/zone — insufficient signal to elevate above noise"], "novelty_flag": true, "confidence_p_real": 0.3, "what_would_change_my_mind": "A second corroborating event (e.g., motion or door contact in the same zone), or a guard radio acknowledgement confirming the flag was meaningful. If board load drops below 6, I'd re-evaluate the suppressed item."}
